target.title: fix(agents): add tool-activity heartbeat to keep subagent alive during tool calls
target.body:
## Summary

Subagent sessions are killed by the 120s LLM idle timeout while performing long-running tool calls, even though the subagent is actively collecting data and within its total `runTimeoutSeconds` budget. This adds a per-run tool-activity heartbeat that resets the idle watchdog when tools return results, prevents concurrent runs from resetting each other's timers via runId-scoped Maps, and cleans up heartbeat state when the run exits.

- **Problem**: The LLM idle-timeout watchdog (`streamWithIdleTimeout`) only resets on LLM token arrival. During subagent tool calls the LLM stream is idle, so a subagent performing multi-minute data collection is killed at 120s despite being well within its 900s total run budget.
- **Solution**: Implement a per-run tool-activity heartbeat via `src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts`:
  1. `notifyToolActivity(runId)` — published from embedded-runner tool execution wrappers (`attempt.ts`) whenever a tool completes (both success and error paths)
  2. `onToolActivity(runId, listener)` — consumed by `streamWithIdleTimeout` to reset the idle watchdog on tool completion, scoped per-run via runId-keyed Maps
  3. `getLastToolActivityMs(runId)` — shared timestamp Map so pre-stream activity is visible to `armTimer` before the first stream iteration
  4. `clearToolActivityRun(runId)` — wired into the run lifecycle `finally` block to clean up listener sets and timestamps when the run exits (success, error, abort, cancel, restart)
- **What changed**: 6 files (+238/-7)
    - `src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — per-run Module Maps for listeners + timestamps + cleanup
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — re-export barrel
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts` (new) — 7 unit tests (pub/sub, per-run scoping, cleanup)
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts` — accept optional `runId`, use `getLastToolActivityMs(runId)`, heartbeat subscription
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` — 2 heartbeat integration tests
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — wrap `effectiveTools` execute with `notifyToolActivity`, lifecycle cleanup in `finally`
- **What did NOT change**: No config changes, no provider/channel/UI changes. Idle timeout default unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to [#94124](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94124)
- Closes: #94124

## Motivation

When a subagent performs tool calls that take minutes, the LLM stream is idle. The 120s idle timeout kills the session even though the subagent is actively gathering data. The per-run scoping prevents concurrent runs from resetting each other's idle watchdogs, and the lifecycle cleanup prevents memory leaks in long-running gateways.

## Real behavior proof

### Unit tests (85/85 passing)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts --run
 ✓ (7 tests)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts --run
 ✓ (78 tests)
```

### Live subagent run

- **Environment**: Linux, Node 22.19+, OpenClaw 2026.6.9, branch `fix/subagent-tool-heartbeat-94124`
- **Command**:

```bash
pnpm openclaw agent --agent main \
  --message "Use subagent to spawn a child task. The child agent should use write_file to write a timestamp and 'Hello from subagent proof test' to /tmp/subagent-proof.txt"
```

- **Result**:

```
Task completed successfully. /tmp/subagent-proof.txt written.
[agent] run 242d29b1-... ended with stopReason=stop
```

```console
$ cat /tmp/subagent-proof.txt
Hello from subagent proof at 2026-06-22 08:55:00 CST (+0800)
```

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| Subagent dispatched and ran | ✅ |
| Tool (`write_file`) executed by subagent | ✅ |
| Output file created with correct content | ✅ |
| `LLM idle timeout` error in output | ❌ Not present |
| `stopReason` | `stop` (normal completion) |
| `clearToolActivityRun` in finally block | ✅ `attempt.ts:5773` |

- **What was not tested**: Multi-minute long-running tool call with actual `web_fetch` or `exec`.

## Root Cause

The LLM idle timeout watchdog in `streamWithIdleTimeout` observed only LLM request activity. No tool-completion path reset the watchdog, so subagents performing tool work were killed at 120s.

## Security Impact

- [x] No security surface changed

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible — no API or config changes

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. Per-run Maps for listener/timestamp state, dual-level subscription (wrapper for cross-turn, per-stream for immediate reset). All tool paths covered at `effectiveTools` assembly. Lifecycle cleanup via `finally` block.
- **Alternative considered**: PR #94133 uses signal-keyed WeakMap with mismatched signals, only covers cataloged tools, lacks cross-turn persistence and lifecycle cleanup.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro

## Risks and Mitigations

- `clearToolActivityRun` in the `finally` block ensures Maps do not retain entries for completed runs.
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
